summary: db-xorma fetches and executes an unpinned, undeclared npm package at runtime
details: When a consumer constructs a Model via Model.create(...), the static resetor() method in dist/index.js attempts to require('db-dx-connector'). If that require fails, the package shells out with execSync(`npm install db-dx-connector --no-warnings --no-save --no-progress --loglevel silent`) and then immediately require()s the freshly installed package and invokes db.queryDBConnect(). The db-dx-connector dependency is not declared in package.json and no version is pinned, so whatever the public registry currently resolves for that name is downloaded and executed inside the consumer's process. This grants whoever controls (or registers) that name arbitrary code execution on any host that uses db-xorma's Model API. A commented-out IIFE in the same file shows the same pattern previously applied to clsx-js (npm uninstall && npm install via execSync), confirming the author's pattern of mutating the consumer's node_modules at runtime.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
